.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/prompt.md:20:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T150207-f2fa11a4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T002613-92f9c760/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260501T195708-a694d2bb/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260501T195708-a694d2bb/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

.ddx/executions/20260417T214435-d2b95ace/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260417T214435-d2b95ace/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>CRM-001</governing>
</context-digest>

Create the initial CRM product framing artifacts from the sparse request "design a CRM".

Assumptions to make explicit in the resulting artifacts unless superseded:
- Primary users: sales reps, sales managers, and CRM admins
- Core workflows: managing contacts/accounts, qualifying leads, tracking opportunities, logging activities, and reviewing pipeline/reporting
- Delivery model: browser-based SaaS with authenticated multi-user access

This bead establishes the first authoritative CRM artifacts so later design and build work have a governing source.

Treat those docs as\nthe source of truth for the workflow contract.\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n```bash\nddx bead ready           # Find available tracked work\nddx bead ready --execution # Find execution-safe work for helix run\nddx bead show \u003cid\u003e       # View issue details\nddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --claim  # Claim work\nddx bead close \u003cid\u003e      # Complete work\nddx bead status          # Check tracker health\nbash tests/helix-cli.sh       # Deterministic HELIX wrapper tests\nbash tests/validate-state-rules.sh # Deterministic state detection contract validation\nbash tests/validate-context-digests.sh # Deterministic context-digest coverage validation\nbash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh # Deterministic demo prompt/fixture validation\nbash tests/validate-pages-demo-recording.sh # Deterministic Pages demo-recording workflow validation\nbash tests/validate-skills.sh # Deterministic HELIX skill package validation\njust test                     # Run all tests (CLI + skills)\nhelix run                     # Run bounded HELIX execution loop\nhelix frame [scope]           # Create product vision, PRD, feature specs\nhelix design [scope]          # Create design document through iterative refinement\nhelix build [selector]        # Run one bounded build pass\nhelix check [scope]           # Decide next HELIX action\nhelix review [scope]          # Fresh-eyes review of recent work\nhelix measure [id|scope]      # Verify bead against acceptance criteria + gates\nhelix report [id|scope]       # Analyze results, create follow-on beads, close cycle\nhelix align [scope]           # Top-down reconciliation audit\nhelix evolve \"requirement\"    # Thread a requirement through the artifact stack\nhelix triage \"Title\"          # Create well-structured tracker issues\nhelix commit [issue-id]       # Commit with HELIX format + build gate\nhelix polish [scope]          # Refine issues before implementation\nhelix next                    # Show recommended next issue\n```\n\n## Operational Guide\n\n### Starting a background run\n\n```bash\nnohup helix run \u003e /tmp/helix-run.stdout.log 2\u003e /tmp/helix-run.stderr.log \u0026\necho $! \u003e /tmp/helix-run.pid\n```\n\nDefaults are reasonable: cross-model review (codex builds, claude reviews),\nreview threshold 100 lines, alignment every 15 cycles, 45-min timeout.\n\n### Monitoring a run\n\n```bash\n# Is it running?\nkill -0 $(cat /tmp/helix-run.pid) 2\u003e/dev/null \u0026\u0026 echo \"RUNNING\" || echo \"STOPPED\"\n\n# Progress\ngrep \"^helix:\" /tmp/helix-run.stderr.log | grep -E \"cycle|complete|tokens\" | tail -10\n\n# Why did it stop?\ngrep \"═══\\|stopping\\|BLOCKER\" /tmp/helix-run.stderr.log | tail -5\n```\n\n### Consolidating related issues\n\nBefore a run, group related issues into epics. The run loop's epic focus\nmode will stay on one epic and batch its children:\n\n```bash\n# Create epic\nepic=$(ddx bead create \"Epic: ...\" --type epic ...)\n# Wire children\nddx bead update hx-child1 --parent $epic\nddx bead update hx-child2 --parent $epic\n# Supersede duplicates\nddx bead update hx-old --superseded-by hx-new\nddx bead close hx-old\n```\n\n### Known operational gotchas\n\n- **Codex sandbox bypasses pre-commit hooks** — the run loop has a build\n  gate that catches broken code, but the agent should also run\n  `lefthook run pre-commit` before pushing (Phase 8 in implementation.md).\n- **Skill YAML front matter** — quote values containing colons or pipes,\n  otherwise codex's skill loader silently rejects them.\n- **`asciinema rec` masks child failures** — the recorder can exit `0` and\n  write a non-empty `.cast` even when the wrapped demo command fails. Pages\n  or demo validation must capture and assert the child exit status explicitly;\n  checking only the recorder exit code or `test -s` on the cast is not enough.\n- **Stale in-progress claims** — after a crashed run, unclaim manually:\n  `ddx bead list --status in_progress --json | ddx jq -r '.[].id'`\n  then `ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --status open --assignee \"\"`\n\n## HELIX Workflow Notes\n\nWhen working on HELIX itself in this repo:\n\n- top-level overview: `workflows/README.md`\n- operator loop and automation: `workflows/EXECUTION.md`\n- tracker conventions: `ddx bead --help` (DDx FEAT-004)\n- command summary: `workflows/REFERENCE.md`\n\nKey rules:\n\n- Use the built-in tracker (`ddx bead`); do not use external issue trackers.\n- For general ready-work detection, use `ddx bead ready`, not manual JSONL parsing.\n- For execution selection or `helix run` reasoning, use `ddx bead ready --execution`.\n- Keep `implementation` single-shot and bounded to one issue per run.\n- Use `check` when the ready queue drains to decide whether to build,\n  design, polish, align, backfill, wait, ask for guidance, or stop.\n- Keep alignment and backfill as separate cross-phase actions:\n  - `workflows/actions/reconcile-alignment.md`\n  - `workflows/actions/backfill-helix-docs.md`\n- Quality ratchets are documented in `workflows/ratchets.md`. Ratchet\n  enforcement scripts and floor fixtures belong in adopting projects, not in\n  this repo. This repo defines the pattern and the integration points in\n  action prompts and enforcers.\n\nThink about HELIX in two layers:\n\n- portable skills packaged from `.agents/skills`\n- the stricter HELIX workflow and CLI contract defined under `workflows/` and\n  executed through `helix`\n\nHELIX operates as a **double helix** — two interleaved cycles:\n\n- **Planning helix**: review → plan → validate (creates and refines beads)\n- **Execution helix**: execute → measure → report (claims beads, does work,\n  records results, creates follow-on beads)\n\nEvery action that modifies files must be governed by a bead (bead-first\nprinciple). Planning-helix beads use `kind:planning` label. Measurement\nresults are recorded on beads. Report creates follow-on beads that feed\nback into the planning helix. See `workflows/EXECUTION.md` for details.\n\n## HELIX Skills\n\nInstalled agent skills mirror CLI commands exactly:\n\n- `helix-run` \u003c-\u003e `helix run`\n- `helix-build` \u003c-\u003e `helix build`\n- `helix-check` \u003c-\u003e `helix check`\n- `helix-align` \u003c-\u003e `helix align`\n- `helix-backfill` \u003c-\u003e `helix backfill`\n- `helix-design` \u003c-\u003e `helix design`\n- `helix-polish` \u003c-\u003e `helix polish`\n- `helix-evolve` \u003c-\u003e `helix evolve`\n- `helix-triage` \u003c-\u003e `helix triage`\n- `helix-next` \u003c-\u003e `helix next`\n- `helix-review` \u003c-\u003e `helix review`\n- `helix-measure` \u003c-\u003e `helix measure`\n- `helix-report` \u003c-\u003e `helix report`\n- `helix-experiment` \u003c-\u003e `helix experiment`\n- `helix-frame` \u003c-\u003e `helix frame`\n- `helix-commit` \u003c-\u003e `helix commit`\n- `helix-worker` — background run monitor (no matching CLI subcommand)\n\nRule: public skill names are `helix-\u003ccommand\u003e`, and `\u003ccommand\u003e` must match the\nCLI subcommand exactly.\nPublished `SKILL.md` files must declare `name` and `description`; add\n`argument-hint` when the mirrored CLI command accepts a trailing positional\nargument such as a scope, selector, issue ID, or goal.\n\n## HELIX CLI\n\nThis repo ships a HELIX CLI (`scripts/helix`) and a Claude Code plugin\nmanifest (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`).\n\n### Primary installation (plugin mode)\n\n```bash\nclaude --plugin-dir /path/to/helix\n```\n\nPlugin mode discovers all skills automatically and resolves shared resources\nvia `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`. No manual installer step is needed.\n\nKey plugin files:\n- Plugin manifest: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`\n- Skills directory: `skills/` (auto-discovered by plugin loader)\n- Shared resources: `workflows/`\n- CLI implementation: `scripts/helix`\n\n### DDx plugin install\n\nFor use in other repos, install via DDx:\n\n```bash\nddx install helix\n```\n\nThis creates:\n- `~/.ddx/plugins/helix` — plugin files\n- Skills into `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.claude/skills`\n\n### Development install\n\nFor working on HELIX itself, use `--local` to create symlinks instead of\ncopies:\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/helix.git\ncd helix\nddx install helix --local . --force\nln -s \"$(pwd)/scripts/helix\" ~/.local/bin/helix\nhelix doctor --fix\n```\n\nThis symlinks the plugin and skills so edits to your checkout are\nimmediately reflected.\n\nAfter installation, verify with `helix doctor`. Use `helix doctor --fix` to\nrepair stale symlinks.\n\nKey paths:\n- CLI implementation: `scripts/helix` (single file, no wrapper)\n- canonical project skill path: `.agents/skills`, `.claude/skills` (symlinks to `skills/`)\n- plugin symlink: `~/.ddx/plugins/helix`\n- user skill paths: `~/.agents/skills`, `~/.claude/skills`\n- `HELIX_ROOT` env var: if set, `scripts/helix` uses it as the repo root\n  instead of resolving from its own location\n\nUseful commands:\n\n```bash\nhelix design auth                     # create design document\nhelix polish                          # refine issues before implementation\nhelix run --review-every 5\nhelix build\nhelix check repo\nhelix align auth\nhelix backfill repo\nhelix next                            # show recommended next issue\nhelix review                          # fresh-eyes review of last commit\nhelix measure ddx-abc123              # verify bead against criteria + gates\nhelix report FEAT-003                 # batch report across scope\nhelix experiment hx-abc123            # one experiment iteration\nhelix experiment --close              # squash-merge and close session\nddx bead create \"Title\" --type task --labels helix,phase:build\nddx bead ready --json            # machine-readable ready queue\nddx bead ready --json --execution # machine-readable execution-safe queue\nddx bead status                  # tracker health summary\n```\n\n`helix run` is the preferred operator loop. It:\n\n- loops only while true ready HELIX execution work exists\n- may route to `helix design` or `helix polish` when supervisory state requires\n  bounded planning or issue refinement before implementation resumes\n- executes one bounded implementation pass at a time\n- may run `helix review` after a successful implementation pass when review\n  automation is enabled\n- runs `check` when the queue drains\n- can run periodic alignment reviews\n\nDo not replace this with an unconditional `while true` loop.\n\n## Demo Recording\n\nDemos live in `docs/demos/helix-*/` and produce asciinema `.cast` files for\nthe microsite. Each demo supports two modes:\n\n| Mode | Env Vars | Description |\n|------|----------|-------------|\n| **Record** | `DEMO_RECORD=1 DEMO_HARNESS=claude` | Runs with real Claude, saves agent responses + project fixtures |\n| **Replay** | `DEMO_HARNESS=virtual` | Replays recorded responses via DDX virtual agent — no API keys |\n\n```bash\n# Record golden responses (requires Claude API key)\ncd /tmp \u0026\u0026 mkdir demo \u0026\u0026 cd demo\nDEMO_RECORD=1 HELIX_DEMO_RECORDING=1 bash /path/to/helix/docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh\n\n# Replay without API keys\ncd /tmp \u0026\u0026 mkdir replay \u0026\u0026 cd replay\nDEMO_HARNESS=virtual HELIX_DEMO_RECORDING=1 bash /path/to/helix/docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh\n```\n\nEach demo stores:\n- `agent-dictionary/*.json` — recorded prompt→response pairs (DDX virtual agent format)\n- `fixtures/` — project files the agent would create, applied during virtual replay\n\nAfter recording, zero the `delay_ms` fields so replay is instant. Use python\n(NOT `ddx jq` — it corrupts multi-byte UTF-8):\n\n```python\nimport json, glob\nfor f in glob.glob('docs/demos/helix-*/agent-dictionary/*.json'):\n    with open(f, 'r') as fh: data = json.load(fh)\n    data['delay_ms'] = 0\n    with open(f, 'w') as fh: json.dump(data, fh, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n```\n\nThe GitHub Actions pages workflow (`pages.yml`) records all 4 demos using the\nvirtual agent and builds the Hugo site — no API keys or Docker needed in CI.\n\n## Testing Requirements for HELIX Changes\n\nIf you change any of the following, run the HELIX wrapper harness:\n\n- `scripts/helix`\n- `scripts/tracker.sh`\n- `workflows/actions/check.md`\n- `workflows/actions/implementation.md`\n- `workflows/actions/reconcile-alignment.md`\n- `workflows/EXECUTION.md`\n- other docs that materially change the HELIX execution contract\n\nIf you change story/project state detection rules or examples, also run the\nstate-rule validator:\n\n- `workflows/state-rules.yml`\n- `workflows/state-machine.yaml`\n- `tests/validate-state-rules.sh`\n- docs that materially change the HELIX state detection contract\n\nIf you change published skill packaging or metadata, also run the skill package\nvalidator:\n\n- `skills/*/SKILL.md`\n- `.agents/skills`\n- docs that change the HELIX skill packaging contract\n\nIf you change context-digest assembly or validation behavior, also run the\ncontext-digest validator:\n\n- `scripts/refresh_context_digests.py`\n- `scripts/validate_context_digests.py`\n- `workflows/references/context-digest.md`\n- `workflows/actions/input.md`\n- `workflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md`\n- `workflows/actions/reconcile-alignment.md`\n- docs that materially change the FEAT-006 context-digest propagation contract\n\nIf you change demo scripts, replay agent fixtures, or demo validation wiring,\nalso run the demo-fixture validator:\n\n- `docs/demos/*/demo.sh`\n- `docs/demos/*/agent-dictionary/*.json`\n- `tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh`\n- `justfile` entries that wire demo validation into shared test lanes\n\nIf you change the Pages demo-recording workflow or its deterministic validator,\nalso run the Pages demo-recording validator:\n\n- `.github/workflows/pages.yml`\n- `.github/workflows/test.yml`\n- `scripts/record_pages_demos.sh`\n- `tests/validate-pages-demo-recording.sh`\n\nIf you change docs that redefine how portable skills and the HELIX workflow\ncontract are presented publicly, run both harnesses.\n\nRequired checks:\n\n```bash\nbash tests/helix-cli.sh\nbash tests/validate-state-rules.sh\nbash tests/validate-context-digests.sh\nbash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh\nbash tests/validate-pages-demo-recording.sh\nbash tests/validate-skills.sh\ngit diff --check\n```\n\nThe wrapper tests are intentionally deterministic:\n\n- they use temporary git workspaces\n- they stub `codex` and `claude`\n- they verify queue draining, periodic alignment, auto-alignment, dry-run\n  output, tracker operations, and launcher installation behavior\n\nPrefer these deterministic tests over live Codex or Claude calls when\nvalidating wrapper behavior.\n\n## Non-Interactive Shell Commands\n\n**ALWAYS use non-interactive flags** with file operations to avoid hanging on confirmation prompts.\n\nShell commands like `cp`, `mv`, and `rm` may be aliased to include `-i` (interactive) mode on some systems, causing the agent to hang indefinitely waiting for y/n input.\n\n**Use these forms instead:**\n```bash\n# Force overwrite without prompting\ncp -f source dest           # NOT: cp source dest\nmv -f source dest           # NOT: mv source dest\nrm -f file                  # NOT: rm file\n\n# For recursive operations\nrm -rf directory            # NOT: rm -r directory\ncp -rf source dest          # NOT: cp -r source dest\n```\n\n**Other commands that may prompt:**\n- `scp` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` for non-interactive\n- `ssh` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` to fail instead of prompting\n- `apt-get` - use `-y` flag\n- `brew` - use `HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1` env var\n\n**`ddx jq` UTF-8 warning:** `ddx jq` corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 characters\n(e.g., em-dash `—`) when rewriting JSON files. Use python for JSON\nmanipulation of files that may contain non-ASCII text.\n\n## Destructive Command Guard\n\nNEVER run these commands without explicit user request:\n\n| Blocked | Safe Alternative |\n|---------|-----------------|\n| `git reset --hard` | `git stash` |\n| `git push --force` | `git push --force-with-lease` |\n| `git clean -f` | `git clean -i` or explicit file removal |\n| `git checkout .` / `git restore .` | Specify files explicitly |\n| `rm -rf /` or broad recursive deletes | Targeted removal with confirmation |\n\nPre-commit hooks must remain enabled. Do not use `--no-verify`.\n\n## Plan-First Development\n\nFor new features or major work:\n\n1. `helix design [scope]` — create comprehensive design document\n2. `helix polish [scope]` — **decompose the plan into implementable beads**, then refine\n3. `helix run` — execute the implementation loop\n\n**Step 2 is mandatory.** Without polish, agents encounter undecomposed epics\nduring build and attempt ad-hoc decomposition, producing poor breakdowns.\n`helix check` enforces this by recommending `POLISH` when plans exist without\ncorresponding beads.\n\n## Metric-Driven Optimization\n\nFor performance tuning, bundle size reduction, or other measurable optimization:\n\n1. Create a `phase:iterate` issue with the optimization goal\n2. `helix experiment [issue-id|goal]` — runs one experiment iteration\n3. The `helix-experiment` skill loops the action autonomously\n4. All existing tests must pass after every iteration — tests are the spec\n5. Prefer simpler solutions; do not add complexity for marginal gains\n6. At session close, the experiment branch is squash-merged and performance\n   ratchet floors are updated if thresholds are met\n\nExperiments are operator-invoked. `helix check` does not auto-dispatch them.\n\n## Issue Tracking with ddx bead\n\n**IMPORTANT**: This project uses the **built-in HELIX tracker** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or external trackers.\n\n### Quick Start\n\n**Check for ready work:**\n\n```bash\nddx bead ready --json\nddx bead ready --json --execution\n```\n\n**Create new issues:**\n\n```bash\nddx bead create \"Issue title\" --type task --description \"Detailed context\" --priority 1\nddx bead create \"Issue title\" --labels helix,phase:build --spec-id TP-036\n```\n\n**Claim and update:**\n\n```bash\nddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --claim\nddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --priority 1\n```\n\n**Complete work:**\n\n```bash\nddx bead close \u003cid\u003e\n```\n\n### Issue Types\n\n- `bug` - Something broken\n- `feature` - New functionality\n- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)\n- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks\n- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)\n\n### Priorities\n\n- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)\n- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)\n- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)\n- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)\n- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)\n\n### Workflow for AI Agents\n\n1. **Check ready work**: `ddx bead ready` shows unblocked issues\n2. **Claim your task**: `ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --claim`\n3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document\n4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue and add dependency\n5. **Complete**: `ddx bead close \u003cid\u003e`\n\n### Important Rules\n\n- Use `ddx bead` for ALL task tracking\n- Use `--json` flag for programmatic use\n- Check `ddx bead ready` before asking \"what should I work on?\"\n- Do NOT create markdown TODO lists\n- Do NOT use external issue trackers\n- Do NOT duplicate tracking systems\n\n## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)\n\n**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.\n\n**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**\n\n1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up\n2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds\n3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items\n4. **PUSH TO GIT REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:\n   ```bash\n   git pull --rebase\n   git push\n   git status  # MUST show \"up to date with origin\"\n   ```\n5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches\n6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed\n7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session\n\n**CRITICAL RULES:**\n- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds\n- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally\n- NEVER say \"ready to push when you are\" - YOU must push\n- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds\n\n\n## /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-helix-3fdba193-20260417T215244-f151207a/CLAUDE.md\n\n# Claude Notes\n\nSee [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for the main repository instructions.\n\nClaude-specific notes:\n\n- prefer `ddx agent run --harness claude` for non-interactive reproducible runs\n- for interactive work, use Claude Code sessions with HELIX slash commands\n  (`/helix-run`, `/helix-build`, etc.)\n- the `helix` CLI dispatches agents via `ddx agent run` internally — avoid\n  calling `claude -p` directly in examples or docs\n- when updating the HELIX operator flow, also check:\n  - `workflows/EXECUTION.md`\n  - `ddx bead --help` (tracker conventions now in DDx FEAT-004)\n  - `workflows/ratchets.md`\n  - `tests/helix-cli.sh`\n\n\nCurrent date: 2026-04-17\nCurrent working directory: /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-helix-3fdba193-20260417T215244-f151207a"},{"role":"user","content":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"helix-3fdba193\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003ehelix-ddx integration harness with contract-matrix exercise and tolerant-parse tests\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nCreate tests/helix-loop-integration.sh (or equivalent under tests/) that drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx emitting scripted execute-loop --json output. Coverage requirements:\u0026#xA;(a) no_ready_work: helix run exits cleanly with no cycle counted\u0026#xA;(b) single success: one results[] entry with status=success, HELIX applies post-cycle to that bead\u0026#xA;(c) mixed success/failure in one drain\u0026#xA;(d) retry-after cooldown: results[] entry with retry_after set; HELIX does not unclaim or blame\u0026#xA;(e) preserve_ref present: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land\u0026#xA;(f) tolerant parsing: unknown top-level or per-result fields MUST be ignored without error\u0026#xA;(g) strict required fields: missing bead_id or status MUST cause a clear structured error, not a silent pass\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;CI exercise of the contract matrix produced by the audit bead: each DDx-owned item with a code-link resolution has a harness test asserting the linked behavior; each new-DDx-bead resolution is tracked as skipped-pending; each HELIX-backport resolution has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.\n    \u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003cacceptance\u003e\ntests/helix-loop-integration.sh runs with exit code 0 on ci.sh and covers the seven enumerated DDx --json response shapes above. Adding a row to CONTRACT-001-audit.md with resolution=\u0026#39;code-link:\u0026lt;path\u0026gt;\u0026#39; triggers a harness test linking to that path; harness fails if the link is broken or the linked behavior is absent. Unknown-field tolerance and missing-required-field strictness are each asserted by at least one fixture with specific stderr/exit-code expectations documented in the test.\n    \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n    \u003clabels\u003ehelix, phase:build, area:testing, area:cli, area:workflow, phase-3-prep\u003c/labels\u003e\n    \u003cmetadata parent=\"helix-e26584d7\" spec-id=\"tests/helix-cli.sh\" base-rev=\"057ff88f6e0512729df9b19f70b13d58e45d29f1\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/20260417T215244-f151207a\"/\u003e\n  \u003c/bead\u003e\n  \u003cgoverning\u003e\n    \u003cref id=\"tests/helix-cli.sh\" path=\"tests/helix-cli.sh\"/\u003e\n  \u003c/governing\u003e\n  \u003cinstructions\u003e\nYou are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;The bead\u0026#39;s \u0026lt;description\u0026gt; and \u0026lt;acceptance\u0026gt; sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## Process\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says \u0026#34;A1 landed X at \u0026lt;path\u0026gt;\u0026#34;, read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;3. **Implement.** Use `edit` for targeted changes to existing files and `write` for brand-new files. Use `read` (not `bash: cat`) to read files. Use `grep` (not `bash: rg`) to search. Use `ls` (not `bash: ls`) for directory listings.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project\u0026#39;s test and lint commands — typically `cargo test -p \u0026lt;crate\u0026gt;` and `cargo clippy -p \u0026lt;crate\u0026gt; -- -D warnings` for Rust, `bun test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;5. **Commit exactly once.** Use `git add \u0026lt;specific-paths\u0026gt;` with the file paths you actually changed — never `git add -A`, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with `[\u0026lt;bead-id\u0026gt;]`. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## If you cannot finish\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;Write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260417T215244-f151207a/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## Quality bar and the review step\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt\u0026#39;s prompt as a `\u0026lt;review-findings\u0026gt;` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;If this prompt already contains a `\u0026lt;review-findings\u0026gt;` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. _ddx_harness_alias_cache[\"$requested\"]=\"$resolved\"\n  printf '%s' \"$resolved\"\n}\n\n# Adversarial agent rotation: reasoning stages use the primary agent,\n# mechanical stages (build, polish, \"auto\" ]]; then\n  alt_agent=\"$(normalize_agent_name \"${HELIX_ALT_AGENT}\")\"\n  [[ \"$alt_agent\" == \"none\" ]] \u0026\u0026 alt_agent=\"\"\nelif [[ \"${agent}\" == \"codex\" ]] \u0026\u0026 command -v claude \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n  alt_agent=\"claude\"\nelif [[ \"${agent}\" == \"claude\" ]] \u0026\u0026 command -v codex \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n  alt_agent=\"codex\"\nelse\n  # For other agents (e.g. ddx-agent / agent), try ddx agent list to find a suitable alternate.\n  # Prefer claude or codex as the adversarial reviewer when available.\n  alt_agent=\"\"\n  if command -v ddx \u0026\u003e/dev/null; then\n    _alt_list=\"$(ddx agent list --json 2\u003e/dev/null || true)\"\n    for _alt_pref in claude codex; do\n      if [[ \"$_alt_pref\" != \"$agent\" ]]; then\n        _alt_avail=\"$(printf '%s' \"$_alt_list\" | ddx jq -r --arg h \"$_alt_pref\" '.[] | select(.name == $h and .available == true) | .name' 2\u003e/dev/null || true)\"\n        if [[ -n \"$_alt_avail\" ]]; then\n          alt_agent=\"$_alt_pref\"\n          break\n        fi\n      fi\n    done\n  fi\nfi\n# review_agent kept as alias for backward compat with --review-agent flag\nreview_agent=\"${HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT:-$alt_agent}\"\ndry_run=0\nquiet=0\nsummary=0\ntotal_tokens=0\n\n# Session log — stderr is copied to a log file for post-hoc inspection.\n# We use a background tee with explicit PID tracking so the tee process\n# is cleaned up on exit.  This prevents $() subshells (used by the test\n# harness) from hanging on an orphan tee that holds the captured pipe open.\nhelix_log_dir=\"${target_root}/.helix-logs\"\nmkdir -p \"$helix_log_dir\"\nhelix_log=\"${helix_log_dir}/helix-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\"\nexec 3\u003e\u00262\nexec 2\u003e \u003e(tee -a \"$helix_log\" \u003e\u00263)\n_helix_tee_pid=$!\n_helix_cleanup() {\n  exec 2\u003e\u00263 3\u003e\u0026-  # restore stderr, close fd 3\n  if [[ -n \"${_helix_tee_pid:-}\" ]]; then\n    kill \"$_helix_tee_pid\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n    wait \"$_helix_tee_pid\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n  fi\n}\ntrap _helix_cleanup EXIT\n\n# Summary-mode helpers: concise output to fd 3, verbose to fd 2 (log only).\n# In normal mode both go to stderr (which tee's to log anyway).\n\n# Current line count in the log file (for referencing ranges)\nlog_line() {\n  wc -l \u003c \"$helix_log\" 2\u003e/dev/null | tr -d ' '\n}\n\n# Write a concise one-liner to the user (fd 3) — always visible\nsummary_line() {\n  local fmt=\"$1\"; shift\n  # shellcheck disable=SC2059\n  printf \"helix: ${fmt}\\n\" \"$@\" \u003e\u00263\n}\n\n# Write verbose detail — goes to log file only in summary mode, stderr in normal mode\nverbose_line() {\n  local fmt=\"$1\"; shift\n  if (( summary )); then\n    # Write directly to log file only (skip fd 3 / user-visible stderr)\n    # shellcheck disable=SC2059\n    printf \"helix: ${fmt}\\n\" \"$@\" \u003e\u003e \"$helix_log\"\n  else\n    # shellcheck disable=SC2059\n    printf \"helix: ${fmt}\\n\" \"$@\" \u003e\u00262\n  fi\n}\n\n\nusage() {\n  cat \u003c\u003cEOF\nUsage:\n  helix run [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--review-every N] [--max-cycles N] [--no-auto-align] [--no-auto-review] [--summary] [--dry-run]\n  helix input [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--autonomy low|medium|high] \"natural language request\"\n  helix frame [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [scope]\n  helix build [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [selector]\n  helix check [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [scope]\n  helix align [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [scope]\n  helix backfill [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [scope]\n  helix design [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--rounds N] [scope]\n  helix polish [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--rounds N] [scope]\n  helix doctor [--fix]\n  helix start [helix run options...]\n  helix stop\n  helix status [--json]\n  helix next\n  helix review [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [scope]\n  helix experiment [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--close] [issue-id|goal]\n  helix evolve [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [--scope S] [--artifact ID] [--from source] \"requirement\"\n  helix commit [--agent \u003charness\u003e] [issue-id]\n  helix triage \"Title\" [--type task|epic|bug|chore] [ddx bead create options...]\n  ddx bead \u003ccommand\u003e [args...]\n  helix help\n\nCommands:\n  run         Loop over ready HELIX work, then check whether to continue\n  doctor      Check installation health; --fix auto-repairs plugin symlinks and skills\n  start       Launch helix run as a background process (PID file written)\n  stop        Kill the background helix run cleanly\n  input       Accept sparse intent and shape governed HELIX work\n  frame       Create or refine product vision, PRD, and feature specs\n  build       Run one bounded implementation pass\n  check       Run the bounded HELIX queue-health check\n  align       Run reconcile-alignment for a scope (default: repo)\n  backfill    Run documentation backfill for a scope (default: repo)\n  design      Create a comprehensive design document through iterative refinement\n  polish      Refine issues before implementation (dedup, coverage, criteria)\n  next        Show recommended next issue\n  review      Fresh-eyes review of recent work\n  experiment  Run one experiment iteration for metric-driven optimization\n  evolve      Thread a new requirement through the artifact stack\n  commit      Commit with HELIX format, run build gate, push, close issue\n  triage      Create a bead via ddx bead create\n\nOptions:\n  --agent           Agent harness to use: codex, claude, ddx-agent,\n                    embedded, or any harness from \\`ddx agent list\\`\n                    (default: ${agent})\n  --claude          Shorthand for --agent claude\n  --codex           Shorthand for --agent codex\n  --ddx-agent       Shorthand for --agent ddx-agent\n  --embedded        Shorthand for --agent embedded\n  --forge           Deprecated alias for the embedded DDx agent harness\n  --model           Explicit model override to pass through to DDx / the harness\n  --effort          Reasoning effort: low, medium, high, max (defaults vary by action)\n  --review-every    Run alignment every N completed implementation cycles\n  --max-cycles      Stop after N completed implementation cycles\n  --no-auto-align   Do not auto-run alignment when check returns ALIGN\n  --no-auto-review  Do not run fresh-eyes review after each implementation\n  --review-threshold N  Skip review for changes below N lines (default: 100)\n  --review-agent    Use a different agent for reviews (default: adversarial alternate)\n  --dry-run         Print the agent command instead of executing it\n  --quiet, -q       Suppress progress output (command echo, prompt sizes)\n  --summary, -s     Concise output with log-file line pointers (implies --quiet)\n\nEnvironment:\n  HELIX_AGENT          Default primary agent (codex, claude, ddx-agent,\n                       or any ddx harness)\n  HELIX_ALT_AGENT      Alternate agent for adversarial rotation (auto-detected)\n  HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT  Agent timeout in seconds (default: 2700)\n  HELIX_MODEL          Optional model override passed through to DDx / the harness\n  HELIX_EFFORT         Optional effort override passed through to DDx / the harness\n  HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT   Override the library root that contains workflows/\n  HELIX_TRACKER_DIR    Override the tracker directory (default: \u003crepo\u003e/.ddx)\n\nAdversarial rotation:\n  Primary agent handles frame/design/evolve/align/review.\n  Alternate agent handles build/polish/check.\n  Concrete model selection stays with DDx or the harness unless you pass --model.\n\nLogs:\n  Session logs are written to .helix-logs/helix-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log\n  in the project root. All stderr output is captured for post-hoc inspection.\n\nNotes:\n  \\`helix run\\` remains a compatibility wrapper for supervisory routing, but\n  build cycles delegate to \\`ddx agent execute-loop --once\\` when that DDx\n  surface is available.\n  \\`helix build\\` remains a compatibility wrapper for one bounded pass and\n  delegates to \\`ddx agent execute-bead\\` after resolving a matching ready bead\n  when that DDx surface is available.\n  Codex runs as \\`codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox exec\\`.\n  Any harness listed by \\`ddx agent list\\` can be passed to --agent.\n  \\`ddx-agent\\`, \\`embedded\\`, and legacy \\`forge\\` are resolved against the live DDx embedded harness name.\n  When HELIX dispatches through DDx, default model selection is delegated to DDx unless you pass an explicit override.\nEOF\n}\n\nneed_cmd() {\n  if ! command -v \"$1\" \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n    echo \"missing required command: $1\" \u003e\u00262\n    exit 1\n  fi\n}\n\nrun_doctor() {\n  local fix=\"${1:-0}\"\n  local ok=0 warn=0 fail=0 fixed=0\n\n  _check() {\n    local name=\"$1\" required=\"${2:-1}\"\n    if command -v \"$name\" \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n      local ver\n      ver=\"$(\"$name\" --version 2\u003e/dev/null || \"$name\" -V 2\u003e/dev/null || \"$name\" version 2\u003e/dev/null || echo \"ok\")\"\n      ver=\"$(printf '%s' \"$ver\" | head -1)\"\n      printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\\n' \"$name\" \"$ver\"\n      ok=$((ok + 1))\n    elif (( required )); then\n      printf '  ✗  %-12s MISSING (required)\\n' \"$name\"\n      fail=$((fail + 1))\n    else\n      printf '  -  %-12s not found (optional)\\n' \"$name\"\n      warn=$((warn + 1))\n    fi\n  }\n\n  _fix_symlink() {\n    local target=\"$1\" link_path=\"$2\" label=\"$3\"\n    if [[ -L \"$link_path\" ]]; then\n      # Resolve both to absolute paths for comparison\n      local resolved_link resolved_target\n      resolved_link=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"$link_path\")\" \u0026\u0026 cd \"$(readlink \"$link_path\")\" 2\u003e/dev/null \u0026\u0026 pwd)\" || resolved_link=\"\"\n      resolved_target=\"$(cd \"$target\" 2\u003e/dev/null \u0026\u0026 pwd)\" || resolved_target=\"$target\"\n      if [[ -n \"$resolved_link\" \u0026\u0026 \"$resolved_link\" == \"$resolved_target\" ]]; then\n        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n        ok=$((ok + 1))\n        return 0\n      elif [[ -d \"$link_path\" ]]; then\n        # Symlink resolves to a valid directory, just not the expected one\n        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n        ok=$((ok + 1))\n        return 0\n      else\n        if (( fix )); then\n          ln -sfn \"$target\" \"$link_path\"\n          printf '  ✓  %-12s %s (updated)\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n          fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n          return 0\n        else\n          printf '  ✗  %-12s %s (stale: points to %s)\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\" \"$(readlink \"$link_path\")\"\n          fail=$((fail + 1))\n          return 1\n        fi\n      fi\n    elif [[ -e \"$link_path\" ]]; then\n      printf '  ✗  %-12s %s exists but is not a symlink\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n      fail=$((fail + 1))\n      return 1\n    else\n      if (( fix )); then\n        mkdir -p \"$(dirname \"$link_path\")\"\n        ln -s \"$target\" \"$link_path\"\n        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s (created)\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n        fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n        return 0\n      else\n        printf '  ✗  %-12s NOT FOUND at %s\\n' \"$label\" \"$link_path\"\n        fail=$((fail + 1))\n        return 1\n      fi\n    fi\n  }\n\n  printf 'HELIX Doctor\\n'\n  printf '============\\n\\n'\n  printf 'Version:  helix %s\\n' \"$HELIX_VERSION\"\n  printf 'Root:     %s\\n' \"$library_root\"\n  (( fix )) \u0026\u0026 printf 'Mode:     --fix (will auto-repair)\\n'\n  printf '\\n'\n\n  printf 'Required:\\n'\n  _check bash 1\n  _check git 1\n  _check ddx 1\n\n  printf '\\nAgent CLIs (at least one required):\\n'\n  local has_agent=0\n  # Use ddx agent list for discovery when available; fall back to hardcoded checks.\n  if command -v ddx \u0026\u003e/dev/null; then\n    local _agent_list\n    _agent_list=\"$(ddx agent list --json 2\u003e/dev/null || true)\"\n    if [[ -n \"$_agent_list\" ]]; then\n      local _name _avail\n      while IFS= read -r _name; do\n        _avail=\"$(printf '%s' \"$_agent_list\" | ddx jq -r --arg h \"$_name\" '.[] | select(.name == $h) | .available' 2\u003e/dev/null || true)\"\n        if [[ \"$_avail\" == \"true\" ]]; then\n          _check \"$_name\" 0; has_agent=1\n        else\n          printf '  -  %-12s not available (ddx agent list reports unavailable)\\n' \"$_name\"\n        fi\n      done \u003c \u003c(printf '%s' \"$_agent_list\" | ddx jq -r '.[].name' 2\u003e/dev/null || true)\n    else\n      # ddx agent list returned nothing — fall back to direct binary checks\n      if command -v claude \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n        _check claude 0; has_agent=1\n      fi\n      if command -v codex \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n        _check codex 0; has_agent=1\n      fi\n    fi\n  else\n    if command -v claude \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n      _check claude 0; has_agent=1\n    fi\n    if command -v codex \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n      _check codex 0; has_agent=1\n    fi\n  fi\n  if (( ! has_agent )); then\n    printf '  ✗  No agent CLI found (need at least one from: ddx agent list)\\n'\n    fail=$((fail + 1))\n  fi\n\n  printf '\\nOptional:\\n'\n  _check tmux 0\n  _check asciinema 0\n  _check hugo 0\n  _check docker 0\n\n  printf '\\nWorkflows:\\n'\n  if [[ -d \"$helix_docs_root\" ]]; then\n    printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\\n' \"workflows\" \"$helix_docs_root\"\n  else\n    printf '  ✗  %-12s NOT FOUND at %s\\n' \"workflows\" \"$helix_docs_root\"\n    fail=$((fail + 1))\n  fi\n\n  # Skills reference .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/ from the target repo.\n  # Verify that path resolves so agents can find workflow docs.\n  printf '\\nPlugin path:\\n'\n  local plugin_dir=\"${target_root}/.ddx/plugins/helix\"\n  _fix_symlink \"$library_root\" \"$plugin_dir\" \"plugin\"\n\n  printf '\\nSkills:\\n'\n  local skill_count=0\n  if [[ -d \"$library_root/skills\" ]]; then\n    skill_count=\"$(find \"$library_root/skills\" -name SKILL.md 2\u003e/dev/null | wc -l)\"\n  fi\n  printf '  %-14s %s skills found in library\\n' \"\" \"$skill_count\"\n\n  # Check .agents/skills and .claude/skills symlinks in target repo\n  printf '\\nSkill symlinks:\\n'\n  local skills_dir=\"${library_root}/skills\"\n  local agents_dir=\"${target_root}/.agents/skills\"\n  local claude_dir=\"${target_root}/.claude/skills\"\n  local stale_links=0\n\n  for skill_src in \"$skills_dir\"/helix-*/SKILL.md; do\n    [[ -e \"$skill_src\" ]] || continue\n    local skill_name\n    skill_name=\"$(basename \"$(dirname \"$skill_src\")\")\"\n    local rel_target=\"../../skills/$skill_name\"\n\n    # .agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e\n    if [[ -L \"$agents_dir/$skill_name\" ]]; then\n      local current\n      current=\"$(readlink \"$agents_dir/$skill_name\")\"\n      if [[ \"$current\" != \"$rel_target\" ]] \u0026\u0026 (( fix )); then\n        ln -sfn \"$rel_target\" \"$agents_dir/$skill_name\"\n        fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n      elif [[ \"$current\" != \"$rel_target\" ]]; then\n        stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))\n      fi\n    elif (( fix )); then\n      mkdir -p \"$agents_dir\"\n      ln -s \"$rel_target\" \"$agents_dir/$skill_name\"\n      fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n    else\n      stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))\n    fi\n\n    # .claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e\n    if [[ -L \"$claude_dir/$skill_name\" ]]; then\n      local current\n      current=\"$(readlink \"$claude_dir/$skill_name\")\"\n      if [[ \"$current\" != \"$rel_target\" ]] \u0026\u0026 (( fix )); then\n        ln -sfn \"$rel_target\" \"$claude_dir/$skill_name\"\n        fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n      elif [[ \"$current\" != \"$rel_target\" ]]; then\n        stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))\n      fi\n    elif (( fix )); then\n      mkdir -p \"$claude_dir\"\n      ln -s \"$rel_target\" \"$claude_dir/$skill_name\"\n      fixed=$((fixed + 1))\n    else\n      stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))\n    fi\n  done\n\n  if (( stale_links \u003e 0 )); then\n    printf '  ✗  %d missing or stale skill symlinks (run helix doctor --fix)\\n' \"$stale_links\"\n    fail=$((fail + 1))\n  else\n    printf '  ✓  .agents/skills and .claude/skills up to date\\n'\n    ok=$((ok + 1))\n  fi\n\n  printf '\\nTracker:\\n'\n  if [[ -f \"$tracker_file\" ]]; then\n    local bead_count\n    bead_count=\"$(wc -l \u003c \"$tracker_file\")\"\n    printf '  ✓  %-12s %s beads\\n' \"$tracker_file\" \"$bead_count\"\n  else\n    printf '  -  %-12s no tracker in this directory\\n' \"$tracker_file\"\n  fi\n\n  printf '\\n'\n  if (( fixed \u003e 0 )); then\n    printf 'Fixed: %d items repaired\\n' \"$fixed\"\n  fi\n  if (( fail \u003e 0 )); then\n    printf 'Result: %s ok, %s warnings, %s FAILURES\\n' \"$ok\" \"$warn\" \"$fail\"\n    (( ! fix )) \u0026\u0026 printf 'Hint: run \"helix doctor --fix\" to auto-repair\\n'\n    return 1\n  else\n    printf 'Result: %s ok, %s warnings — ready to go\\n' \"$ok\" \"$warn\"\n    return 0\n  fi\n}\n\ntracker_init() {\n  ddx bead init \u003e/dev/null\n}\n\ntracker_ensure() {\n  need_cmd ddx\n\n  if [[ ! -f \"$tracker_file\" ]]; then\n    tracker_init\n  fi\n}\n\nrequire_tracker() {\n  tracker_ensure\n}\n\n# Strip non-JSON advisory lines from ddx output before piping to JSON consumers.\n# Advisory lines (e.g. upgrade notices) appear before the JSON and must not\n# reach ddx jq or other JSON parsers. Skips all lines until the first JSON\n# delimiter ([ or {), then passes all subsequent lines unchanged. This handles\n# both compact and pretty-printed JSON as well as array and object output.\n# Used by tracker_exec when --json is requested.\nstrip_advisory() {\n  awk 'found || /^[{[]/ { found=1; print }' || true\n}\n\ntracker_exec() {\n  local has_json=0\n  for arg in \"$@\"; do\n    [[ \"$arg\" == \"--json\" ]] \u0026\u0026 { has_json=1; break; }\n  done\n  if (( has_json )); then\n    ddx bead \"$@\" | strip_advisory\n  else\n    ddx bead \"$@\"\n  fi\n}\n\nrefresh_context_digest_for_bead() {\n  local bead_id=\"$1\"\n  local helper=\"${library_root}/scripts/refresh_context_digests.py\"\n\n  [[ -f \"$helper\" ]] || return 0\n  command -v python3 \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261 || return 0\n\n  python3 \"$helper\" \\\n    --apply \\\n    --root \"$target_root\" \\\n    --library-root \"$library_root\" \\\n    --bead \"$bead_id\" \u003e/dev/null\n}\n\ntracker_create() {\n  local title=\"\" type=\"task\" labels=\"\" deps=\"\" parent=\"\" spec_id=\"\"\n  local description=\"\" design=\"\" acceptance=\"\" priority=2 execution_eligible=\"\"\n  local superseded_by=\"\" replaces=\"\"\n  local -a cmd=(ddx bead create)\n\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n      --type)       type=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --labels)     labels=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --deps)       deps=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --parent)     parent=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --spec-id)    spec_id=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --description) description=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --design)     design=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --acceptance) acceptance=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --priority)   priority=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --execution-eligible) execution_eligible=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --superseded-by) superseded_by=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --replaces)   replaces=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --set)        cmd+=(--set \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --silent)     shift ;;\n      -h|--help|help)\n        tracker_exec create --help\n        return 0\n        ;;\n      *) title=\"$1\"; shift ;;\n    esac\n  done\n\n  if [[ -z \"$title\" ]]; then\n    echo \"tracker: title required\" \u003e\u00262\n    return 1\n  fi\n\n  cmd+=( \"$title\" --type \"$type\" --priority \"$priority\" )\n  [[ -n \"$labels\" ]]      \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--labels \"$labels\")\n  [[ -n \"$parent\" ]]      \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--parent \"$parent\")\n  [[ -n \"$description\" ]] \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--description \"$description\")\n  [[ -n \"$acceptance\" ]]  \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--acceptance \"$acceptance\")\n\n  [[ -n \"$spec_id\" ]]     \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--set \"spec-id=$spec_id\")\n  [[ -n \"$design\" ]]      \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--set \"design=$design\")\n  [[ -n \"$superseded_by\" ]] \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--set \"superseded-by=$superseded_by\")\n  [[ -n \"$replaces\" ]]    \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--set \"replaces=$replaces\")\n\n  if [[ -z \"$execution_eligible\" ]]; then\n    case \",${labels},\" in\n      *,phase:build,*|*,phase:deploy,*|*,phase:iterate,*)\n        execution_eligible=\"true\" ;;\n      *)\n        execution_eligible=\"false\" ;;\n    esac\n  fi\n  [[ -n \"$execution_eligible\" ]] \u0026\u0026 cmd+=(--set \"execution-eligible=$execution_eligible\")\n\n  local id\n  id=\"$(\"${cmd[@]}\")\" || return 1\n\n  if [[ -n \"$deps\" ]]; then\n    local dep\n    for dep in ${deps//,/ }; do\n      ddx bead dep add \"$id\" \"$dep\" \u003e/dev/null || return 1\n    done\n  fi\n\n  refresh_context_digest_for_bead \"$id\" || return 1\n\n  printf '%s\\n' \"$id\"\n}\n\ntracker_show() {\n  tracker_exec show \"$@\"\n}\n\ntracker_update() {\n  if [[ \"${1:-}\" == \"--help\" || \"${1:-}\" == \"-h\" || \"${1:-}\" == \"help\" ]]; then\n    tracker_exec update --help\n    return 0\n  fi\n\n  local id=\"$1\"\n  shift\n\n  local -a cmd=(ddx bead update \"$id\")\n  local claim=0 unclaim=0 deps_to_add=\"\"\n\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n      --title)      cmd+=(--title \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --status)     cmd+=(--status \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --priority)   cmd+=(--priority \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --labels)     cmd+=(--labels \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --acceptance) HELIX does not\npredict which bead DDx will select.\n\nDDx owns **loop, selection, and execution**: bead selection, managed worktree\nexecution, close-with-evidence, retry suppression, and orphan recovery.\n\nThe intended adoption end state is: after each\n`ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` call, HELIX parses `results[].bead_id`\nand `results[].status`, then applies post-cycle supervisory policy to the bead\nDDx actually executed, not a pre-selected bead.\n\n### Current compatibility behavior\n\n- `helix run` keeps HELIX-owned supervisory routing, queue-health decisions,\n  post-build review/alignment hooks, and persisted run state, while delegating\n  each build cycle to `ddx agent execute-loop --once`.\n- `helix build` delegates the managed execution attempt to\n  `ddx agent execute-bead` for explicit single-bead execution.\n- Epic focus: HELIX sets `execution-eligible: false` on non-child beads to bias\n  DDx's deterministic ordering toward epic children; no DDx flag needed.\n- Current gap: the wrapper still pre-selects a bead and exports\n  `HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE` before DDx chooses the actual execution target. That is\n  a temporary internal seam, not part of the HELIX/DDx contract.\n- Current gap: review and periodic alignment still run as wrapper-owned\n  post-cycle hooks. The queued-bead model is the target state, but it is not\n  yet the shipped wrapper behavior.\n- Current gap: the wrapper still carries legacy retry/backoff, skip-tracking,\n  and blocker-report logic. The durable contract remains DDx-owned cooldown via\n  `execute-loop-retry-after` plus tracker visibility through `ddx bead blocked`.\n\nInterpret `check` as follows:\n\n- `NEXT_ACTION: BUILD`\n  More safe ready work exists; continue.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: DESIGN`\n  Run `helix design \u003cscope\u003e` once, then re-run `check`.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: POLISH`\n  Run `helix polish \u003cscope\u003e` once to decompose plans and refine issues, then\n  re-run `check`.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: ALIGN`\n  Run `reconcile-alignment` once for the indicated scope if auto-alignment is\n  enabled, then re-run `check`.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: BACKFILL`\n  Stop and hand off to `backfill-helix-docs` for the indicated scope.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: WAIT`\n  Stop. Do not attempt to build around the blocker or auto-unblock it.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: GUIDANCE`\n  Stop and get user or stakeholder input.\n- `NEXT_ACTION: STOP`\n  No actionable work remains for the current scope.\n\n`helix run` is a bounded compatibility controller, not a repair loop.\n\n- It counts only completed build passes toward `--max-cycles`.\n- Contract target: parse `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`, use\n  `results[].bead_id` for all post-cycle bookkeeping, and avoid any private\n  selector handoff that DDx does not expose.\n- It may dispatch `helix design` or `helix polish` before build when\n  supervisory state indicates missing design authority, undecomposed plans,\n  or stale issue refinement.\n- It may run fresh-eyes review after a successful build when review\n  automation is enabled; `--no-auto-review` disables that post-implementation\n  review.\n- It may switch to `--review-agent` for cross-model verification during review.\n- It may run `reconcile-alignment` every `N` completed implementation passes\n  when `--review-every N` is set; `--no-auto-align` disables that post-drain\n  alignment step.\n- It may persist run-controller state for `helix status` including focused epic,\n  attempt counters, cycle timing, and token totals.\n- It should refresh `.helix/context.md` at run start, on epic switch, and\n  every 5 completed implementation passes so long-lived sessions keep current\n  build/test commands and tracker counts in view.\n- It may stay on a focused epic until all children are done, then run a scoped\n  post-epic review before leaving that scope. Epic focus uses `execution-eligible`\n  curation, not DDx selection flags.\n- Queue drift (superseded-by, parent change, spec-id change) is caught at `helix check`\n  time before the bead enters the ready queue;
