{\n  local _ddx_out _ddx_rc=0\n  _ddx_out=\"$(command ddx \"$@\")\" || _ddx_rc=$?\n  printf '%s\\n' \"$_ddx_out\" | grep -v '^💡\\|^⬆️' || true\n  return \"$_ddx_rc\"\n}\nexport -f ddx\n\ntest_count=0\n\nfail() {\n  echo \"FAIL: $*\" \u003e\u00262\n  exit 1\n}\n\nassert_eq() {\n  local expected=\"$1\" actual=\"$2\" msg=\"$3\"\n  if [[ \"$expected\" != \"$actual\" ]]; then\n    printf 'expected:\\n%s\\nactual:\\n%s\\n' \"$expected\" \"$actual\" \u003e\u00262\n    fail \"$msg\"\n  fi\n}\n\nassert_contains() {\n  local haystack=\"$1\" needle=\"$2\" msg=\"$3\"\n  if [[ \"$haystack\" != *\"$needle\"* ]]; then\n    printf 'missing substring: %s\\nin:\\n%s\\n' \"$needle\" \"$haystack\" \u003e\u00262\n    fail \"$msg\"\n  fi\n}\n\nassert_not_contains() {\n  local haystack=\"$1\" needle=\"$2\" msg=\"$3\"\n  if [[ \"$haystack\" == *\"$needle\"* ]]; then\n    printf 'unexpected substring: %s\\nin:\\n%s\\n' \"$needle\" \"$haystack\" \u003e\u00262\n    fail \"$msg\"\n  fi\n}\n\n# ── Shared mock factory ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nmake_mock_bin() {\n  local root=\"$1\"\n  local real_ddx\n  real_ddx=\"$(type -P ddx)\"\n\n  mkdir -p \"$root/bin\"\n\n  # Mock ddx: supports execute-loop --json with scripted responses.\n  # Scripted responses come from files in $root/state/ (written by fixtures).\n  cat \u003e\"$root/bin/ddx\" \u003c\u003c'MOCK'\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\nreal_ddx=\"__REAL_DDX__\"\nstate_root=\"${MOCK_STATE_ROOT:?}\"\n\nrecord_execute() {\n  printf '%s\\n' \"$1\" \u003e\u003e \"$state_root/ddx-calls.log\"\n}\n\npick_issue() {\n  if [[ -n \"${HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE:-}\" ]] \\\n     \u0026\u0026 \"$real_ddx\" bead show \"$HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE\" --json \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\n    printf '%s\\n' \"$HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE\"\n    return 0\n  fi\n  \"$real_ddx\" bead ready --json --execution 2\u003e/dev/null \\\n    | \"$real_ddx\" jq -r '.[0].id // empty'\n}\n\ndispatch_managed_execution() {\n  local harness=\"$1\" issue_id=\"$2\"\n  # For integration tests: simulate a zero-delayed agent that always succeeds.\n  # The mock always \"succeeds\" — the execute-loop JSON response drives assertions.\n  record_execute \"dispatch $issue_id via $harness\"\n}\n\n# Load scripted execute-loop response from state file if present.\n# Falls back to compatibility mode (pick_issue + dispatch).\nhandle_execute_loop() {\n  shift 2  # drop \"agent execute-loop\"\n  local harness=\"codex\" json_mode=0\n  local -a remaining=()\n\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n      --harness) harness=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --json)   json_mode=1;  shift ;;\n      --once|--model|--effort|--from|--poll-interval) shift ;;\n      *)        remaining+=(\"$1\"); shift ;;\n    esac\n  done\n\n  local issue_id\n  issue_id=\"$(pick_issue)\"\n  record_execute \"execute-loop ${issue_id:-none}\"\n\n  # Serve scripted JSON response if the fixture wrote one.\n  local script_file=\"$state_root/execute-loop-response.json\"\n  if (( json_mode )) \u0026\u0026 [[ -f \"$script_file\" ]]; then\n    cat \"$script_file\"\n    # Remove the script so subquent calls in the same fixture can switch modes.\n    rm -f \"$script_file\"\n    exit 0\n  fi\n\n  if [[ -z \"$issue_id\" ]]; then\n    (( json_mode )) \u0026\u0026 printf '{\"processed\":0,\"results\":[]}\\n'\n    exit 0\n  fi\n\n  # Non-JSON compatibility path (used by helix-cli.sh's non-integration tests).\n  dispatch_managed_execution \"$harness\" \"$issue_id\"\n  exit $?\n}\n\n# Stub execute-bead for compatibility with the build path.\nhandle_execute_bead() {\n  shift 2  # drop \"agent execute-bead\"\n  local issue_id=\"${1:-}\"; shift || true\n  local harness=\"codex\"\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n      --harness) harness=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      --json|--model|--effort|--from|--prompt|--no-merge) shift ;;\n      *) shift ;;\n    esac\n  done\n  record_execute \"execute-bead ${issue_id:-none}\"\n  dispatch_managed_execution \"$harness\" \"${issue_id:-none}\"\n  exit $?\n}\n\nif [[ \"${1:-} ${2:-}\" == \"agent execute-loop\" ]]; then\n  if [[ \" $*\" == *\" --help \"* ]]; then\n    exec \"$real_ddx\" \"$@\"\n  fi\n  handle_execute_loop \"$@\"\nfi\n\nif [[ \"${1:-} ${2:-}\" == \"agent execute-bead\" ]]; then\n  if [[ \" $*\" == *\" --help \"* ]]; then\n    exec \"$real_ddx\" \"$@\"\n  fi\n  handle_execute_bead \"$@\"\nfi\n\nexec \"$real_ddx\" \"$@\"\nMOCK\n\n  # Fix the real_ddx path substitution in the heredoc.\n  sed -i \"s|__REAL_DDX__|${real_ddx}|g\" \"$root/bin/ddx\"\n  chmod +x \"$root/bin/ddx\"\n\n  # Mock codex / claude (unused in integration tests but expected by helix).\n  cat \u003e\"$root/bin/codex\" \u003c\u003c'MOCK'\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\necho \"mock codex: $*\"\nMOCK\n  chmod +x \"$root/bin/codex\"\n\n  cat \u003e\"$root/bin/claude\" \u003c\u003c'MOCK'\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\necho \"mock claude: $*\"\nMOCK\n  chmod +x \"$root/bin/claude\"\n}\n\nmake_workspace() {\n  cd \"$repo_root\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n  local root\n  root=\"$(mktemp -d)\"\n  mkdir -p \"$root/work\"\n  make_mock_bin \"$root\"\n  (\n    cd \"$root/work\"\n    git init -q\n  )\n  echo \"$root\"\n}\n\n# Seed the built-in tracker with N ready issues.\nseed_tracker() {\n  local root=\"$1\" count=\"${2:-0}\"\n  mkdir -p \"$root/work/.ddx\"\n  local i\n  for ((i = 0; i \u003c count; i++)); do\n    printf '{\"id\":\"hx-loop-%d\",\"title\":\"loop issue %d\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"status\":\"open\",\"priority\":2,\"labels\":[\"helix\",\"phase:build\",\"kind:build\"],\"parent\":\"\",\"spec-id\":\"\",\"description\":\"\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance\":\"\",\"dependencies\":[],\"owner\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"execution-eligible\":true,\"superseded-by\":\"\",\"replaces\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}\\n' \"$i\" \"$i\"\n  done \u003e \"$root/work/.ddx/beads.jsonl\"\n}\n\n# Run helix inside the workspace, with all env wired up.\nrun_helix() {\n  local root=\"$1\"; shift\n  local cmd=\"${1:-help}\"; shift || true\n  (\n    cd \"$root/work\"\n    HOME=\"$root/home\" \\\n    PATH=\"$root/bin:$PATH\" \\\n    MOCK_STATE_ROOT=\"$root/state\" \\\n    HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT=\"$repo_root/workflows\" \\\n    HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL=1 \\\n    HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT=\"codex\" \\\n    HELIX_ALT_AGENT=\"none\" \\\n    HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=0 \\\n    DDX_BEAD_DIR=\"$root/work/.ddx\" \\\n    DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1 \\\n    bash \"$repo_root/scripts/helix\" \"$cmd\" --quiet \"$@\"\n  )\n}\n\n# Capture stdout+stderr separately for assertions.\nrun_helix_capture() {\n  local root=\"$1\"; shift\n  local cmd=\"${1:-help}\"; shift || true\n  (\n    cd \"$root/work\"\n    HOME=\"$root/home\" \\\n    PATH=\"$root/bin:$PATH\" \\\n    MOCK_STATE_ROOT=\"$root/state\" \\\n    HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT=\"$repo_root/workflows\" \\\n    HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL=1 \\\n    HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT=\"codex\" \\\n    HELIX_ALT_AGENT=\"none\" \\\n    HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=0 \\\n    DDX_BEAD_DIR=\"$root/work/.ddx\" \\\n    DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1 \\\n    bash \"$repo_root/scripts/helix\" \"$cmd\" --quiet \"$@\" 2\u003e\u00261\n  )\n}\n\n# ── Test runner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nrun_test() {\n  local name=\"$1\"; shift\n  local start_time\n  start_time=\"$(date +%s.%N)\"\n  (( test_count++ ))\n  printf '%s\\n' \"  [$test_count] $name\"\n  if \"$@\" 2\u003e\u00261; then\n    local elapsed\n    elapsed=\"$(date +%s.%N)\"\n    printf '    ✓ passed (%.2fs)\\n' \"$(echo \"$elapsed - $start_time\" | bc)\"\n  else\n    printf '    ✗ FAILED\\n'\n    exit 1\n  fi\n}\n\n# =====================================================================\n# FIXTURES\n# =====================================================================\n\n# ── (a) no_ready_work ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_no_ready_work_exits_cleanly() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 0  # no ready work\n\n  local stdout stderr rc\n  # No issues → pick_issue returns empty → execute-loop returns {\"processed\":0}\n  # HELIX should stop after one check-and-drain pass with 0 cycles.\n  # Patch the mock so pick_issue also sees no issues.\n  # (The workspace is already seeded with 0 issues.)\n\n  stderr=\"$(run_helix_capture \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2\u003e/dev/null)\" || true\n\n  # Should NOT crash.\n  [[ \"$rc\" -eq 0 ]] || true\n\n  # No \"implementation\" dispatched.\n  local ddx_calls\n  ddx_calls=\"$(cat \"$root/state/ddx-calls.log\" 2\u003e/dev/null || echo '')\"\n\n  # execute-loop should be called once (drain pass) but no dispatch.\n  assert_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"execute-loop\" \\\n    \"execute-loop should be called even when no work is ready (drain attempt)\"\n  assert_not_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"dispatch\" \\\n    \"no_ready_work: execute-loop should return without dispatching\"\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# ── (b) single success ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_single_success_applies_post_cycle() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 1\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return a single success.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 1,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-0\",\n      \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 1500\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  run_helix \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align \u003e/dev/null\n\n  local ddx_calls\n  ddx_calls=\"$(cat \"$root/state/ddx-calls.log\")\"\n\n  # execute-loop should be invoked.\n  assert_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"execute-loop hx-loop-0\" \\\n    \"single success: execute-loop should be called with the bead\"\n\n  # Post-cycle: the bead should be closed.\n  local bead_status\n  bead_status=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null \\\n    | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n  assert_eq \"closed\" \"$bead_status\" \\\n    \"single success: HELIX should close the bead post-cycle\"\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# ── (c) mixed success/failure ───────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_mixed_success_failure_per_bead_post_cycle() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 2\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return mixed results.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 2,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-0\",\n      \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 1200\n    },\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-1\",\n      \"status\": \"failure\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 3000,\n      \"error\": \"implementation returned non-zero\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  run_helix \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align \u003e/dev/null\n\n  local ddx_calls\n  ddx_calls=\"$(cat \"$root/state/ddx-calls.log\")\"\n\n  assert_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"execute-loop\" \\\n    \"mixed: execute-loop should be called\"\n\n  # hx-loop-0 should be closed; hx-loop-1 should NOT be closed.\n  local s0 s1\n  s0=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n  s1=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-1 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n  assert_eq \"closed\" \"$s0\" \"mixed: successful bead should be closed\"\n  assert_eq \"open\"   \"$s1\" \"mixed: failed bead should NOT be auto-closed by HELIX\"\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# ── (d) retry-after cooldown ─────────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_retry_after_cooldown_does_not_unclaim() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 1\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return a retry-after result.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 1,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-0\",\n      \"status\": \"failure\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 800,\n      \"retry_after\": \"2029-12-31T23:59:59Z\",\n      \"reason\": \"execution timed out\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  run_helix \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align \u003e/dev/null\n\n  local ddx_calls\n  ddx_calls=\"$(cat \"$root/state/ddx-calls.log\")\"\n  assert_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"execute-loop hx-loop-0\" \\\n    \"retry-after: execute-loop should be called\"\n\n  # The bead should NOT be auto-closed (cooldown is DDx's job).\n  local bead_status\n  bead_status=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n  assert_eq \"open\" \"$bead_status\" \\\n    \"retry-after: HELIX should not auto-close a cooldown bead\"\n\n  # The bead should remain claimed (HELIX should not unclaim it either).\n  local claimed_pid\n  claimed_pid=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.\"claimed-pid\" // empty')\"\n  #claimed_pid may be empty if unclaimed; the key assertion is it should NOT\n  #be forcibly unclaimed by HELIX (safe_unclaim guards closed beads only).\n  #Since execute-loop owns the retry_after state, HELIX should leave it alone.\n  assert_not_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"unclaim\" \\\n    \"retry-after: HELIX should not explicitly unclaim a cooldown bead\"\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# ── (e) preserve_ref \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"preserve_ref\": \"feat/keep-this-work\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 900\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  local stderr\n  stderr=\"$(run_helix_capture \"$root\" run ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_tolerant_parsing_unknown_fields_ignored() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 1\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return results with completely unknown fields\n  # at both top-level and per-result level.  HELIX must not error.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 1,\n  \"totally_unknown_top_field\": \"should be ignored\",\n  \"another_nonsense\": 99999,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-0\",\n      \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 500,\n      \"zzz_custom_agent_field\": \"hello\",\n      \"ddx_internal_trace_id\": \"abc123\",\n      \"nested_junk\": {\"foo\": \"bar\", \"baz\": [1,2,3]}\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  local stderr rc=0\n  stderr=\"$(run_helix_capture \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2\u003e/dev/null)\" || rc=$?\n\n  # Should exit cleanly even with unknown fields.\n  [[ \"$rc\" -eq 0 ]] || {\n    # If it fails, stderr should NOT be a JSON parse error.\n    if [[ \"$stderr\" == *\"JSON\"* || \"$stderr\" == *\"parse\"* || \"$stderr\" == *\"unexpected\"* ]]; then\n      printf 'Unexpected parse error from tolerant fixture:\\n%s\\n' \"$stderr\" \u003e\u00262\n      fail \"tolerant parsing: unknown fields caused a parse error\"\n    fi\n  }\n\n  # Execute-loop should still have been invoked.\n  local ddx_calls\n  ddx_calls=\"$(cat \"$root/state/ddx-calls.log\")\"\n  assert_contains \"$ddx_calls\" \"execute-loop\" \\\n    \"tolerant parsing: execute-loop should be called\"\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# ── (g) strict required fields ──────────────────────────────────────\n\ntest_missing_bead_id_causes_structured_error() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 1\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return a result missing bead_id.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 1,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 500\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  local stderr rc=0\n  stderr=\"$(run_helix_capture \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2\u003e/dev/null)\" || rc=$?\n\n  # Should fail (missing bead_id is a required field).\n  [[ \"$rc\" -ne 0 ]] || {\n    # If it accidentally passes, the behavior must be documented:\n    # either it produces a structured error in output OR it closes nothing.\n    # The contract requires a clear structured error, not silent pass.\n    local bead_status\n    bead_status=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n    if [[ \"$bead_status\" == \"closed\" ]]; then\n      fail \"strict required fields: missing bead_id must cause an error, not silently close the bead\"\n    fi\n    fail \"strict required fields: missing bead_id must cause non-zero exit or structured error\"\n  }\n\n  # Error output should reference the missing field or bead_id.\n  if [[ \"$stderr\" != *\"bead_id\"* \u0026\u0026 \"$stderr\" != *\"required\"* \u0026\u0026 \"$stderr\" != *\"missing\"* ]]; then\n    printf 'Expected error about missing bead_id; got:\\n%s\\n' \"$stderr\" \u003e\u00262\n    fail \"strict required fields: error should mention the missing field (bead_id)\"\n  fi\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\ntest_missing_status_causes_structured_error() {\n  local root\n  root=\"$(make_workspace)\"\n  seed_tracker \"$root\" 1\n\n  # Script execute-loop to return a result missing status.\n  cat \u003e\"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json\" \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n{\n  \"processed\": 1,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-loop-0\",\n      \"duration_ms\": 500\n    }\n  ]\n}\nEOF\n\n  local stderr rc=0\n  stderr=\"$(run_helix_capture \"$root\" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2\u003e/dev/null)\" || rc=$?\n\n  # Should fail (missing status is a required field).\n  [[ \"$rc\" -ne 0 ]] || {\n    local bead_status\n    bead_status=\"$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2\u003e/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')\"\n    if [[ \"$bead_status\" == \"closed\" ]]; then\n      fail \"strict required fields: missing status must cause an error, not silently close the bead\"\n    fi\n    fail \"strict required fields: missing status must cause non-zero exit or structured error\"\n  }\n\n  # Error output should reference the missing field or status.\n  if [[ \"$stderr\" != *\"status\"* \u0026\u0026 \"$stderr\" != *\"required\"* \u0026\u0026 \"$stderr\" != *\"missing\"* ]]; then\n    printf 'Expected error about missing status; got:\\n%s\\n' \"$stderr\" \u003e\u00262\n    fail \"strict required fields: error should mention the missing field (status)\"\n  fi\n\n  rm -rf \"$root\"\n}\n\n# =====================================================================\n# CONTRACT MATRIX — code-link resolution verification\n# =====================================================================\n\n# CONTRACT-001-audit.md rows with resolution='code-link:\u003cpath\u003e':\n# Each row's linked behavior is asserted below. data.text: There is already a CRM example artifact stack under `docs/examples/crm/`, and it explicitly says the scope decision is meant to be recorded by this bead. I’m going to update that stack and the parent epic consistently rather than creating a separate decision mechanism.

data.output:
---
ddx:
  id: crm.readme
---

# CRM Example — Greenfield Frame Artifacts

This directory holds frame-phase artifacts for a hypothetical CRM product,
authored from the sparse intake `"design a CRM"` via the HELIX methodology.

It exists for two reasons:

1. **Dogfood.** Exercise HELIX templates on a greenfield product distinct from
   HELIX itself.
2. **Demo material.** Provide a self-contained example of how HELIX turns a
   one-line intake into vision/PRD artifacts with explicit assumptions.

## Artifacts

| ID | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `crm.vision` | `00-discover/product-vision.md` | North-star CRM product vision |
| `crm.prd` | `01-frame/prd.md` | CRM product requirements (MVP scope) |

## Scope and authority

These artifacts govern downstream CRM work referenced under the provisional
spec-id `CRM-001`. Beads filed against `CRM-001` should adopt `crm.prd` (or a
narrower canonical ID once feature specs land) as their governing reference.

The scope decision — whether the CRM artifact stack remains here as an example
or migrates to a dedicated product repo — is tracked separately by bead
`helix-5129f35d` ("Resolve CRM request scope against current HELIX repo
vision"). Until that resolves, treat this directory as a greenfield planning
exercise hosted inside the HELIX repo, not a HELIX product surface.

## Status

Frame artifacts only. Design (`02-design/`) and downstream phases are not
authored here yet; those are tracked by sibling beads `helix-8944c622` (design)
and `helix-7e8a9c4b` (decomposition).

1. **Audience**: Small B2B sales teams (2–25 reps) — not enterprise; not
   consumer; not transactional / e-commerce CRM.
2. **Primary users**: Sales reps, sales managers, and CRM admins (no
   marketing, no support, no finance personas in MVP).
3. **Core workflows**: Managing contacts/accounts, qualifying leads,
   tracking opportunities, logging activities, reviewing pipeline.
4. **Delivery model**: Browser-based SaaS with authenticated multi-user
   access (no native mobile, no self-hosted).
5. **Geography/language**: USD + English-only for MVP.
6. **Multi-tenancy**: One workspace per customer; strict isolation between
   workspaces.
7. **Pricing model not yet decided** — has no impact on MVP functional
   scope but affects seat-management UX.
8. **Data scale**: Workspaces ≤100k records for MVP performance budgets.
9. **The repo decision is open**: This artifact stack lives in the HELIX
   repo as a planning exercise; whether it migrates to a dedicated CRM repo
   is the subject of bead `helix-5129f35d`.

### Dependencies

- Design-phase ADRs for stack, hosting, and auth (downstream).
- Bead `helix-5129f35d` (CRM repo-scope decision) — does not block frame
  work but governs where subsequent phases land.

## Risks

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| MVP scope creeps to match enterprise CRM expectations | High | High | Non-goals list is enforced as a frame-phase artifact; new requirements must displace existing P0/P1 or move to P2 |
| Defaults don't fit any real customer because we tried to please everyone | Medium | High | Anchor defaults to one well-understood ICP (small B2B sales) and document the choice; resist "configurable for everyone" pull |
| Spreadsheet-using teams don't switch because the cost of change > the cost of bad data | Medium | High | Time-to-first-value metric is a release gate; onboarding flow is treated as P0 |
| Stack choice prematurely commits before design phase | Low | Medium | All stack selection deferred to ADRs; PRD lists only what affects requirements |
| Data export not credible → procurement blocks adoption | Low | High | R-8 (CSV export) is P0; document the export contract before launch |

## Open Questions

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation:
- Core intent: create the governed planning/design work needed for a new CRM product.

data.text: The initial “move it” read is weaker now that the repo already contains `docs/examples/crm/` as a HELIX example artifact stack. The tighter decision is: CRM stays here only as a worked example/demo planning artifact, not as a HELIX product feature or domain extension; downstream product implementation would move to a dedicated repo later.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "ddx bead update helix-db7d13a9 --notes \"Scope decision from helix-5129f35d: CRM work remains in this HELIX repository only as a worked example/demo planning artifact stack rooted at docs/examples/crm/. This is consistent with docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md because HELIX ships methodology content, artifact templates, quality criteria, and examples; CRM is not being added as a HELIX product surface or domain-specific extension. If the CRM effort proceeds beyond example/demo planning into product implementation, migrate it to a dedicated HELIX-managed CRM project repository by copying docs/examples/crm/ as the new repo's initial governing artifacts and replacing provisional spec-id CRM-001 with that repo's canonical IDs.\""

/bin/zsh -lc 'ddx bead update helix-5129f35d --notes "Decision: keep CRM work in this repository only as a HELIX worked example/demo planning artifact stack at docs/examples/crm/. Rationale: docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md defines HELIX as methodology content plus artifact catalog/templates/examples and one routing/alignment skill; it explicitly keeps domain-specific product extensions out of scope. Therefore CRM artifacts may remain here as illustrative example material, but not as a HELIX product surface. Migration trigger: if work shifts from example/demo planning into CRM product implementation, create a dedicated HELIX-managed CRM project repository and seed it from docs/examples/crm/, then retire or supersede CRM-001 beads in this repo."'

data.text: I’ve recorded the decision on both the current escalation bead and its parent epic. Next I’m updating the CRM example docs so the artifact stack no longer says the repo decision is open.

+templates, quality criteria, and examples; it does not add domain-specific
+product extensions. CRM planning here is therefore illustrative material, not a

Title:    Design a CRM

Desc:     <context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>

<governing>CRM-001</governing>
</context-digest>

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation:
- Core intent: create the governed planning/design work needed for a new CRM product.
- Artifact layer affected: frame and design, with downstream decomposition into execution-ready work.
- Explicit constraints: none beyond high autonomy.

Current repo search found no existing CRM artifact stack or matching open bead. The HELIX repo's governing PRD is about HELIX itself, so a separate escalation bead records the repo-scope conflict. Until that is resolved, treat this as greenfield CRM planning work managed through HELIX.

Working assumptions for downstream beads:
- B2B web CRM for a small-to-mid-size sales team
- MVP includes contacts, accounts, leads/opportunities, tasks/activities, notes, pipeline visibility, and basic reporting
- Multi-user SaaS posture; implementation architecture still to be designed
Accept:   1. A governed CRM artifact stack is created through HELIX, starting with frame artifacts and continuing into design and decomposition work.
2. Downstream beads use provisional spec-id CRM-001 until authoritative CRM frame artifacts establish canonical IDs.
3. Scope assumptions created from the sparse intake are explicit rather than hidden.

+# CRM Scope Decision
+
+Bead: `helix-5129f35d`
+Parent epic: `helix-db7d13a9`
+Governing scope: `helix.prd` at `docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md`
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep the CRM artifact stack in this repository only as a HELIX worked example
+and demo planning artifact rooted at `docs/examples/crm/`.
+
+## Rationale
+
+`docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md` defines HELIX as methodology content, an artifact
+catalog, examples, and one portable alignment/routing skill. It also keeps
+domain-specific product extensions out of scope. The CRM artifacts are
+therefore acceptable here as illustrative HELIX material, but not as a HELIX
+product surface or CRM product implementation.
+
+## Parent-Epic Linkage
+
+The parent epic `helix-db7d13a9` remains the umbrella for the CRM example
+planning stack under provisional spec-id `CRM-001`. If work proceeds beyond
+example/demo planning into actual CRM product implementation, migrate the
+artifact stack to a dedicated HELIX-managed CRM project repository by seeding
+that repo from `docs/examples/crm/`, then supersede or retire the `CRM-001`
+beads that no longer belong in this repository.
